Patch 095: recovers a Cohere tool name carried under a string `function` key. The post-094 A/B scored one such action wrong at 64801 tokens -- the model emitted a correct call and the server dropped it, surfacing raw markup in content with finish_reason stop. 091 covered only tool_name and tool_call_id.
Patch 091's detector test landed in /data/sgl-v0515; the patch created it but it had never been written to the live tree, so its six cases had never run there

Post-095 three-seed ladders for both FP8 ships: Laguna 21/21 seed-rungs through 245,279 actual tokens, North-Mini 21/21 through 245,172; zero clamps, shortfalls, errors, or budget-bound rungs. Prompts byte-identical across seeds. Neither ship shows a measurable agentic ceiling below the 262,144 context limit.
Chart contract reworked to per-row sampling/seeds with conservative all-seeds-pass aggregation; README, benchmarks/README, and FINDINGS corrected off the pre-fix claims.

Prompt-profile control re-measured on the post-095 chain with the committed generator: texture effect reproduced in aggregate (6/6 heterogeneous vs 1/6 repeated), zero parser drops remaining, every failure a genuine model failure.
remaining_order: Make profile-control calibration replayable: record filler_chars per prompt and accept pinned counts on replay, so a re-run is a controlled A/B rather than a fresh draw. Blocks isolating any serving change in that control; needs a prompts-row schema field and a matching relaxation of the chart loader's strict row-key check.

items.next_action: Two of three done. (1) Native-FP8 profile COMPLETE 2026-07-19: decode is led by dense rocBLAS GEMM (~38-42% of non-collective GPU time), not attention (~20-28%); the run also surfaced the extend/agentic-turn tax now tracked as R97-J, and the unexplained rocBLAS residue as R97-K. (2) KV-split resweep COMPLETE 2026-07-19: 64 holds. (3) REMAINING: correctness-gated gfx1201 tuner for the dense block-FP8 kernel at Laguna's 39x shared-expert shapes (N,K)=(512,2048) and (2048,256). The stock SGLang tuner is unsafe here -- it benchmarks a kernel serving does not select, applies no numerical gate, and issues OOB loads at K=256.

items.next_action: Cut the agentic turn tax at depth. Laguna pays 604.6 ms of TTFT to append ONE token to a 176,588-token cache hit, and 607.7 ms to append 64 -- the cost is the prefix walk, not the suffix. Cause is the extend launch grid (extend_attention.py:63) whose third dimension is cdiv(max_len_extend, BLOCK_M); with BLOCK_M=64 on gfx1201 a short suffix collapses it to 1, so ~24 workgroups per rank walk the whole prefix while decode splits the same walk 64 ways. Evaluate two arms: (a) route small-suffix cache-hit extends through the decode attention path, (b) add a KV-split dimension to extend. Gate on numerical equivalence before any speed claim.
test_gate: Isolated kernel reproducer at Laguna's real shapes matches the current extend output within tolerance
measure_extend_cost.py shows a TTFT reduction at 117K and 176K with cache_hit_verified true
Decode TPOT at the same depths is unchanged (no regression on the path that already works)
256K agentic tool-use ladder still passes 21/21 across three seeds
profile_decode_kernels.py --phase prefill_extend confirms the extend attention share dropped

items.next_action: Attribute the four large Cijk_* decode kernels (~38-42% of non-collective decode time) to named modules and shapes. LEADING HYPOTHESIS, not confirmed: the residue is intended -- Laguna-XS.2-FP8's quantization_config.ignore excludes lm_head, all five self_attn projections (q/k/v/o/g_proj), and mlp.gate, so those dense GEMMs have no FP8 weights and fall back to BF16 rocBLAS/Tensile; the retained Tensile names carry a BBS_BH (bf16/fp32-compute) datatype code. Confirm or refute from launch dims in the preserved traces -- NOT from the MT128x128x32 tile name, which is the macro-tile and not the GEMM shape. Second, independent lead: at decode batch 1 these are M=1 GEMV-shaped calls running a 128x128 throughput tile. Promotion to the FP8 path is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE and is an accuracy decision, not a profiling one.
test_gate: Each large Cijk_* decode kernel is attributed to a named module and shape, evidenced by launch or input dims rather than the tile name
Per-rank kernel times reconcile with per-step firing counts (per-layer projections fire 40x per step; lm_head once)
The BBS_BH datatype reading is confirmed against this rocBLAS build or dropped from the argument
The profile receipt's open_question and not_established[0] are replaced with the finding or an explicit remaining-open statement
receipts: /data/profiling-traces/laguna-native-2026-07-19/ (raw traces, both arms and ranks, preserved off volatile /tmp)
